zh.lifeSummary: 當Susan Rowland於大約1482年出生在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德洛斯賀波利時，她的父親John Rowland II31歲，母親Agnes Hansen16歲。她於大約1503年與John H Taylor of Shadoxhurst在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1582年在英國諾圖波藍德享年101歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德洛斯賀波利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Susan Rowland于大约1482年出生在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德洛斯賀波利时，她的父亲John Rowland II31岁，母亲Agnes Hansen16岁。她于大约1503年与John H Taylor of Shadoxhurst在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1582年在英国諾圖波藍德享年101岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德洛斯賀波利。

nameMeanings.name: Rowland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Rollant, Rolant, Rolent, Roulent (ancient Germanic Hrodland, Rodland, from hrōd ‘glory’ + land ‘land’), introduced into England by the Normans. It was made famous by French romances about Roland, the most famous of the peers of Charlemagne who was killed at Roncesvalles in AD 778. Although widely used across post-Conquest England (along with Oliver, the name of Roland's companion), it seems to have been restricted to particular gentry families and was never popular. Compare Roland .

English: habitational name from Rowland (Derbyshire) or Rowland Wood in Slinfold (Sussex). The Derbyshire placename derives from Old Norse rá ‘roe, roe buck’ or rá ‘land mark, boundary’ + lúndr ‘small wood, grove’. The Sussex placename probably derives from Middle English roughe ‘rough’ + lond ‘land’ (Old English rūh, land).
English: in northern England and perhaps elsewhere, perhaps a post-medieval variant of   Rawling  . Compare   Rawlinson  ,   Rollinson  .

variants.name: Roland
meanings: French, Walloon, German, English, and Scottish: from an ancient Germanic personal name composed of hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + land ‘land’. In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the more common French variant Rolland . See also English Rowland .

Norwegian (also Røland): habitational name from any of several farms named Rødland mainly in Hordaland or Røyland mainly in Agder, most of them from Old Norse reyrr ‘heap of stones’ or reyrr ‘rush’ (a plant) + the second element land ‘(piece of) land, farmstead’. Compare Rolland .

Norwegian: habitational name from the farm name Roland in Lillehammer, with the first element Old Norse ruth ‘clearing’, or Roland in Agder around Lindesnes, with the first element possibly the Old Norse personal name Rói (either a short form of names beginning with Ro(d)- ‘fame, honor’ or composed with a word for ‘giant’ or ‘pagan priest’). The second element of the farm names is land ‘(piece of) land, farmstead’.

variants.name: Rollison
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal names Rouland or Roulin; see Rowland , Rolling . Alternatively, and perhaps more likely, the name may be a post-medieval pronunciation of Rawlinson .

variants.name: Orlando
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Rocco, Sal, Vito, Antonio, Carlo, Marco, Santo, Carmine, Giovanni, Cosimo.
Italian: from the personal name Orlando, earlier Rolando (see Rowland ).

variants.name: Reuland
meanings: German: from a personal name, the German form of Roland (see Rowland ).

variants.name: Rolling
meanings: English: variant of Rowland , with -ling substituted for -land.

English: habitational name from Rowling Court in Goodnestone (Kent) or Rowling Street in Bilsington (Kent). The origins of these placenames are uncertain, but they may both derive from an unattested Old English word related to an ancient Germanic base rull- ‘something round’ + the Old English placename forming suffix -ing.
English: from the rare Middle English personal name Ro(u)lin, a diminutive of either Roll (Rolf) or Rol(l)and. See Rolfe and Rowland .

variants.name: Rowlands
meanings: English and Welsh: variant of Rowland , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Rowlett
meanings: English: from a diminutive of the personal name Rol(l)and. See Rowland and compare Rolling .
English: from a pet form of the personal name   Ralph  . Compare   Rawling  .
English: from a pet form of the personal name Rolf. See Rolfe and compare Rolling .

variants.name: Rowlan
meanings: English (South London): variant of Rowland with loss of -d.

variants.name: Rouland
meanings: English: variant of   Rowland  .
Americanized form of Norwegian   Rauland  .
In some cases possibly also French: variant of   Roland  .

nameMeanings.name: Susan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: vernacular form of   Susanna .  Among well-known bearers are the American film stars  Susan Hayward  (  1918–75 ) and  Susan Sarandon  (  b. 1946  as  Susan Tomalin ).

variants.name: Suzan
meanings: vernacular form of   Susanna .  Among well-known bearers are the American film stars  Susan Hayward  (  1918–75 ) and  Susan Sarandon  (  b. 1946  as  Susan Tomalin ).

variants.name: Susanna
meanings: New Testament form (Luke 8:3) of the Hebrew name Shoshana (from shoshan ‘lily’, which in modern Hebrew also means ‘rose’).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: When Susan Rowland was born about 1482, in Rothbury, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, her father, John Rowland II, was 31 and her mother, Agnes Hansen, was 16. She married John H Taylor of Shadoxhurst about 1503, in Northumberland, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1582, in Northumberland, England, at the age of 101, and was buried in Rothbury, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé John H Taylor of Shadoxhurst environ 1503, à Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1582, à Northumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 101 ans, et a été enterrée à Rothbury, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Susan Rowlandが約1482年にイギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドロスブリーで生まれた時，父John Rowland IIは31歳，母Agnes Hansenは16歳であった。約1503年にイギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドでJohn H Taylor of Shadoxhurstと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1582年にイギリスノースウンバーランドで101歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドロスブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Susan Rowland(이)가 대략 1482년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 로스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Rowland II(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes Hansen(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 대략 1503년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드에서 John H Taylor of Shadoxhurst(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1582년 영국 노르스벌랜드에서 101세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 로스버이에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Susan Rowland родилась около 1482 г. в местности ротбури, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John Rowland II было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Agnes Hansen, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John H Taylor of Shadoxhurst около 1503 г. в местности нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1582 г. в местности нортумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 101 год и была похоронена в местности ротбури, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания.
